avalancheActivity.highlights: The avalanche danger is within the upper range of danger level 3 (considerable). The conditions are precarious for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities outside marked and open pistes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Large quantities of fresh snow and the wind-drifted snow remain in some cases prone to triggering.
In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent and larger, in particular along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland.
Avalanches can be released by a single winter sport participant. Caution is to be exercised on steep slopes, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Especially on very steep slopes and on wind-loaded slopes more medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural avalanches are possible. Here the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach very large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
70 to 110 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Wednesday above approximately 1800 m.
5 to 10 cm of snow will fall until Saturday above approximately 1400 m. The large surface-area wind slabs will be covered with new snow in some cases and therefore difficult to recognise.
The weather conditions gave rise to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack. The large quantity of fresh snow and the often deep wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering. Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Numerous large and, in isolated cases, very large natural dry avalanches have been released since Wednesday.

tendency.highlights: Wind and new snow. The meteorological conditions will facilitate a slow strengthening of the snowpack. The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Old wind slabs will be covered with new snow in some cases and therefore difficult to recognise.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The older wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant or triggered naturally. The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain and on steep slopes. At elevated altitudes these avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.

Additionally avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Thursday: The sometimes storm force wind has transported the new snow significantly. The various wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on shady slopes.

10 to 15 cm of snow will fall until midday above approximately 1400 m.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Large-grained weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The large quantity of fresh snow and in particular the widespread wind slabs can be released very easily.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 30 to 40 cm of snow, and up to 50 cm in some localities, will fall until midday. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong northeasterly wind, sometimes deep wind slabs will form in particular on southwest, south and southeast facing slopes.

In particular sunny slopes as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls: New snow and wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.

The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in all aspects and generally above the tree line.

Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In particular adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas and high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: In the last four days hard wind slabs formed.

In particular sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The old snowpack is hard and its surface has a melt-freeze crust.

In the areas described above, the new wind slabs will bond poorly with the old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The new snow and wind slabs can be released very easily.
avalancheActivity.comment:
20 to 30 cm of snow will fall from midday. Above approximately 1000 m snow will fall.
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind from easterly directions, sometimes deep wind slabs will form.
Wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain and on steep shady slopes above approximately 2000 m.

In some places avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In the last few days hard wind slabs formed over a wide area. The sometimes storm force wind has transported the new snow significantly. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind from easterly directions, sometimes deep wind slabs will form. The wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.

Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The new snow and wind slabs can be released very easily.
avalancheActivity.comment:
20 to 30 cm of snow will fall from midday. Above approximately 1000 m snow will fall.
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind from easterly directions, sometimes deep wind slabs will form.
Wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain and on steep shady slopes above approximately 2000 m.

In some places avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In the last few days hard wind slabs formed over a wide area. The sometimes storm force wind has transported the new snow significantly. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind from easterly directions, sometimes deep wind slabs will form. The wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.

Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The fresh snow and very particularly the wind slabs can be released very easily above the tree line.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The old wind slabs will be covered with new snow in some cases and therefore difficult to recognise. These can as before be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach large size. This applies in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, and on wind-loaded slopes above the tree line.

Wind-loaded slopes where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially dangerous. Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes. In some places various wind slab layers are lying on surface hoar.
In some places avalanches can also release deeper layers of the snowpack and reach very large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until midday above approximately 1400 m.

In the last few days hard wind slabs formed over a wide area. The sometimes storm force wind has transported the new snow significantly. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

The new snow and wind slabs of the last few days are lying on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.

Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The snowpack will be quite prone to triggering. At intermediate and high altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail. As a consequence of the strong northerly wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent as the day progresses.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in some places be released easily. New snow and wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach very large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are capable of being triggered.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh and older wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow will fall until midday above approximately 1400 m. Soft wind slabs will form.
The somewhat older wind slabs will be covered with new snow in some cases and therefore difficult to recognise. These can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases.
The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack above approximately 2000 m. At elevated altitudes these avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.

Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes. In very isolated cases avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow will fall until midday above approximately 1400 m. Soft wind slabs will form.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind-loaded slopes where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are dangerous.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind, avalanche prone wind slabs formed on Thursday. In particular on extremely steep slopes and adjacent to ridgelines more medium-sized natural avalanches are to be expected in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place. Mostly these are shallow.
In some places the avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size.
Even single winter sport participants can release avalanches. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Tuesday above approximately 1800 m.
5 to 10 cm of snow, and up to 20 cm in some localities, will fall until Saturday above approximately 1400 m. The wind slabs will be covered with new snow in some cases and therefore difficult to recognise.
The weather conditions gave rise to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack. The strong wind has transported the new snow significantly.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: Wind and new snow. The meteorological conditions will facilitate a slow strengthening of the snowpack. The avalanche danger will persist.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: At elevated altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.
The avalanche-prone wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of the precipitation. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground. They can be released by a single winter sport participant.

snowpackStructure.comment: The fresh and older wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mostly sunny.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In little used terrain a sometimes treacherous avalanche situation will prevail. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack can be released easily by individual winter sport participants especially in little used terrain. Mostly they are medium-sized. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible in isolated cases.

As a consequence of the strong wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days, in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain above approximately 2200 m. The soft wind slabs are only small but in some cases prone to triggering. They are clearly recognisable to the trained eye.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of a strong wind from westerly directions, mostly small wind slabs formed in the last few days especially in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in some places.

Above the tree line: The snowpack will be prone to triggering. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Below the tree line as well as steep sunny slopes: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a crust that is barely capable of bearing a load.

tendency.highlights:
Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

Some snow will fall until Sunday in particular in the north. The mostly small wind slabs of the last few days will be covered with new snow in some cases and therefore difficult to recognise. As a consequence of a strong northerly foehn wind, further wind slabs will form in the south.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger. Wind slabs require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Saturday above approximately 1800 m. Some mostly small natural avalanches are not ruled out. The wind will be strong over a wide area. Over a wide area large surface-area wind slabs will form. These will form in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1900 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.
Weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach medium size. They can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign. The avalanche prone locations are quite prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. In particular on very steep shady slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are possible.
In particular on very steep slopes and in gullies and bowls medium-sized avalanches are possible.
Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended. Experience and restraint are required.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow of last week is lying on surface hoar above the tree line. Weak layers deep in the old snowpack are difficult to recognise. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger. In addition the wind slabs should be taken into account.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size in isolated cases.
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released easily or naturally. The avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow and are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow will fall on Saturday over a wide area. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies especially on west, north and east facing slopes. The snowpack remains generally prone to triggering.
At high altitude the wind slabs have increased in size additionally in the last two days.
The snowpack will be moist at low and intermediate altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche danger will persist. Above the tree line a treacherous avalanche situation will prevail.

avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche-prone wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of the precipitation. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground.

snowpackStructure.comment: Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions gave rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mostly sunny.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wind-loaded slopes where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially unfavourable. Avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls small and medium-sized slab avalanches are possible. In particular transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are treacherous.

snowpackStructure.comment: Below approximately 1800 m only a little snow is lying. In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. In the last few days sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In some places wind slabs are lying on a hard crust. Light snowfall above approximately 1600 m: In the course of the day further wind slabs will form in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. These are mostly small.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies especially on steep shady slopes adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls above the tree line. As a consequence of the occasionally strong wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form. These are in some cases prone to triggering. In some cases avalanches are medium-sized.

Apart from the danger of being buried, restraint should be exercised in particular in view of the danger of avalanches sweeping people along and giving rise to falls. Only a small amount of snow is lying for the time of year.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The fresh and older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack at elevated altitudes. Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

Low and intermediate altitudes: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is barely capable of bearing a load.

avalancheActivity.comment: On very steep slopes hardly any loose snow avalanches are possible, but they will be mostly small.

snowpackStructure.comment: A little new snow above approximately 1500 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. The wind slabs can be released in isolated cases in particular on steep shady slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
The fresh snow and in particular the mostly small wind slabs that are forming in particular adjacent to ridgelines can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The snowpack will be moist at low altitude.
